methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Cuby Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Inversion Space
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Cobod
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Material Evolution, a Series A-stage housing and construction technology company founded by Liz Gilligan and Sam Clark, has raised $15M to date. The valuation landscape for this sector remains constrained: while four private peers operate in Housing & Construction (ICON, Cuby Technologies, Inversion Space, and Cobod), no public comparables exist, and the private peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage companies to support a reliable peer-median methodology.

The analysis therefore relies on stage-progression analysis, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage advancement. Using the $15M total raised as a valuation proxy, this method implies a $30M post-money valuation. Given the single-method foundation and absence of tight peer data for triangulation, the supported range is $26M to $34M, with $30M as the median point. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result, a conservative band appropriate given methodology limitations.

Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.0x multiplier for frontier construction tech and the absence of detailed comparable financials (revenue, growth, runway, capital intensity) from the peer set. A peer-median approach remains inaccessible until more same-stage Housing & Construction companies achieve funding visibility or Material Evolution's own operating metrics (ARR, unit economics) become available for relative valuation anchoring.
